response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role demands deep hardware/SoC expertise — performance architecture for AI accelerators, IP modeling, deep neural network optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no evidence of SoC modeling, AI accelerator work, DNN training/inference optimization, or chip architecture experience. The educational requirement (PhD+3, MS+6, or BS+8 in a relevant engineering/CS field) is potentially met via the MIT BS plus 13+ years experience, but the technical domain mismatch is severe.
strengths: Strong 13+ year software engineering track record with low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic
BS Computer Science from MIT meets the educational baseline
Some C/C++ embedded exposure (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, wave field synthesis hardware project)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip architecture experience
No demonstrated work in AI accelerators, deep learning workload optimization, or GPU architecture
Career trajectory is web/application full-stack, not hardware performance architecture at principal IC level

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
Long engineering tenure with low-latency systems exposure
